# Label Guide

Keep the label set small — apply one **area** label + one **type** label
(+ a priority label when it matters) to every issue and PR.

## Area
| Label | Used for |
|---|---|
| `backend` | FastAPI service, auth, DB, APIs |
| `frontend` | Next.js app, UI screens |
| `ai` | Parsing, embeddings, RAG pipeline, generators |
| `infra` | Docker, CI/CD, deployment |
| `docs` | Anything under `/docs` |

## Type
| Label | Used for |
|---|---|
| `bug` | Something is broken |
| `enhancement` | New feature or improvement |
| `chore` | Maintenance, deps, refactors with no behavior change |
| `question` | Needs discussion before work starts |

## Priority
| Label | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `priority:high` | Blocks another teammate or a phase deadline |
| `priority:medium` | Should land this phase |
| `priority:low` | Nice to have, can slip |

## Other
| Label | Used for |
|---|---|
| `good-first-issue` | Small, well-scoped — good for Gaurav while ramping up |
| `needs-review` | PR is ready for a teammate to review |
